steps.title: Identify the antecedent and what the blank needs to do
steps.content: The blank fills in a possessive determiner before “peer autonomous Soviet socialist republics.” The noun being possessed is those peer republics; the possessor is the Tajik Republic, a singular entity referred to throughout the passage. The blank must be a singular possessive determiner — not a plural one, and not a contraction.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘their’: A plural possessive determiner — does not agree with the singular antecedent “the Tajik Republic.” ✗
  • Choice B — ‘they’re’: A contraction of “they are” — a verb phrase, not a possessive determiner at all. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘its’: The singular possessive determiner, agreeing in number with the singular “the Tajik Republic.” ✓
  • Choice D — ‘it’s’: A contraction of “it is” or “it has” — a verb phrase, not a possessive determiner. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
